# Project plan

## Start-up phase
<!--
Covering the planning phase, this section should provide a relatively
detailed plan of how work will start on the project. This section is
important because projects need to get up and running quickly.


 - [ ] Setting up collaboration platform inc. code for contributors etc.
 - [ ] License decisions
 - [ ] Reporting framework
-->

Most of the project start-up phase has been already delivered, this includes:

- GitHub page as the collaboration and reporting framework.
- MIT License, as per the original implementation is MIT licensed.

In light of the above, the following activities will be performed during this phase:

- Code of conduct for contributors.
- GitHub discussion page.

## Technical delivery
<!--
Covering the actual delivery of the project this section should
provide at least a high-level description of the implementation.

Including target dates is really important as you need to be committed M
and the ISC need to have a means of tracking delivery
-->

| Month | Milestone | Deliverable |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| 1 | Community setup | GitHub Discussions open for feedback and Code of Conduct |
| 1 | Complete documentation | Full documentation for `Router`, `Route`, and `Layer` in `routing` |
| 2-3 | Package website | `pkgdown`/`altdoc` site for `routing` with guides and real-world examples |
| 4–5 | Cookies parser middleware | `cookies` package developed and documented |
| 6 | CRAN release | `cookies` submitted to CRAN |
| 6 | Final report | Submit final report to the R Consortium summarising deliverables, outcomes, and lessons learned |


## Other aspects

<!--
How will you ensure that your work is available to the widest number
of people? Please specify the open-source or creative commons license(s)
you will use, how you will host your code so that others can contribute,
and how you will publicize your work. We encourage you to plan content
to be shared quarterly on the R Consortium blog.

 - [ ] Announcement post
 - [ ] Delivery blog post
 - [ ] Social media
 - [ ] UseR!
 - [ ] ISC meetings
-->

To make our project reach as many people as possible, we will:

- Write posts and share updates on social media throughout the project.
- Reach out to R web framework authors to present our work, showing them how `routing` can
  improve their frameworks.



## Budget & funding plan

<!--
Note that the maximum grant is $20k USD. The proposal should be in USD.

Outline the milestones for development and how much funding will be
required for each stage (as payments will be tied to project milestone
completion). This also helps the ISC in the event of partial funding. 
Each milestone should specify the work to be done and the
expected outcomes, providing enough detail for the ISC to understand
the scope of the project work and assess the likelihood of success.

We expect that most of the budget will be allocated
for labor costs. We do not cover indirect costs. The ISC grants cannot
cover such things as travel, lodging, food, journal publication fees,
or personal hardware. Cloud services may be covered if they are specific
to the project and the project period. The ISC reserves the right to vet
how funds are used for each project separately. If in doubt, please reach
out to us.
-->

| Milestone | Hours | Cost |
|-----------|-------|------|
| Phase 1: Documentation, package website, and community setup (Months 1–3) | 100 | $2,000 |
| Phase 2: `cookies` middleware development, CRAN submission and report back to R Consortium(Months 4–6) | 100 | $2,000 |
| **Total** | **200** | **$4,000** |
